29#include "pinocchio.h"
30
31// #define BL_GRANDISSIMA
32
33#ifdef PRECISE_TIMING // SI PUO` TOGLIERE?
34
35#define SET_WTIME cputime.partial = MPI_Wtime();
36#define ASSIGN_WTIME(INIT, ACC) \
37 do \
38 { \
39 double ttt = MPI_Wtime(); \
40 cputime.ACC = ttt - cputime.INIT; \
41 } while (0)
42#define ACCUMULATE_WTIME(INIT, ACC) \
43 do \
44 { \
45 double ttt = MPI_Wtime(); \
46 cputime.ACC += ttt - cputime.INIT; \
47 } while (0)
48#else
49#define SET_WTIME
50#define ASSIGN_WTIME(INIT, ACC)
51#define ACCUMULATE_WTIME(INIT, ACC)
52#endif
53
54int initialize_fft(void);
55int init_cosmology(void);
56int generate_densities(void);
57int set_plc(void);
58#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
59int set_scaledep_GM(void);
60#endif
61unsigned int gcd(unsigned int, unsigned int);
62int set_fft_decomposition(void);
63
64int initialization()
65{
66
67 /* timing */
68 cputime.init = MPI_Wtime();
69
70 /* this is for gsl integration */
71 workspace = gsl_integration_workspace_alloc(NWINT);
72 /* this is the initialization of the random number generator */
73 random_generator = gsl_rng_alloc(gsl_rng_ranlxd1);
74
75 /* reading of parameters from file and few other parameter initializations */
76 if (set_parameters())
77 return 1;
78
79 /* call to cosmo.c: initialization of cosmological functions */
80 if (initialize_cosmology())
81 return 1;
82
83 /* initialize pfft and fftw functions */
84 if (initialize_fft())
85 return 1;
86
87 /* set the properties of grids and initialize FFTW quantities, including vectors */
88 if (set_grids())
89 return 1;
90
91 /* computes the smoothing radii */
92 if (set_smoothing())
93 return 1;
94
95 /* now it re-initializes the variance with a top-hat filter */
96 WindowFunctionType = 2;
97 if (initialize_MassVariance())
98 return 1;
99
100 /* initializes quantities needed for the on-the-fly reconstruction of PLC */
101 SET_WTIME;
102 if (set_plc())
103 return 1;
104 ASSIGN_WTIME(partial, set_plc);
105
106 /* computes the number of sub-boxes for fragmentation */
107
108 SET_WTIME;
109 if (set_subboxes())
110 return 1;
111 ASSIGN_WTIME(partial, set_subboxes);
112
113#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
114 /* computes the growth rates for displacements */
115 if (set_scaledep_GM())
116 return 1;
117#endif
118
119#ifdef MASS_MAPS
120 /* initialize mass sheets (needs cosmology + outputs) */
121 if (mass_maps_init_sheets())
122 return 1;
123 /* write sheet table */
124 mass_maps_write_sheet_table();
125#endif
126
127 /* checks that parameters and directives are coherent */
128 if (check_parameters_and_directives())
129 return 1;
130
131 /* estimates the size of output file */
132 if (estimate_file_size())
133 return 1;
134
135 /* this barrier is set to have correct stdout in case the run cannot start */
136 fflush(stdout);
137 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
138
139 /* allocations of memory for fmax and memory tests */
140 SET_WTIME;
141 if (allocate_main_memory())
142 return 1;
143 ASSIGN_WTIME(partial, memory_allocation);
144
145 /* initialization of fft plans */
146 SET_WTIME;
147 if (compute_fft_plans())
148 return 1;
149 ASSIGN_WTIME(partial, fft_initialization);
150
151 /* generation of initial density field */
152 if (!params.ReadProductsFromDumps) /* no generation if products are read from dumps */
153 if (generate_densities())
154 return 1;
155
156 cputime.init = MPI_Wtime() - cputime.init;
157
158 if (!ThisTask)
159 {
160 dprintf(VMSG, ThisTask, "[%s] initialization done, initialization cpu time = %14.6f\n", fdate(), cputime.init);
161 dprintf(VMSG, ThisTask, "\t\t set subboxes time = %14.6f\n"
162 "\t\t set plc time = %14.6f\n"
163 "\t\t memory allocation time = %14.6f\n"
164 "\t\t fft initialization time = %14.6f\n"
165 "\t\t density generation time = %14.6f\n",
166 cputime.set_subboxes, cputime.set_plc, cputime.memory_allocation,
167 cputime.fft_initialization, cputime.dens);
168 }
169
170 return 0;
171}
172
173int initialize_fft(void)
174{
175
176#ifdef USE_FFT_THREADS
177 // if ( internal.nthreads_fft < 0 )
178 internal.nthreads_fft = internal.nthreads_omp;
179 if (internal.nthreads_fft > 1)
180 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using %d threads for FFTs\n", internal.nthreads_fft);
181#endif
182
183 /* Initialize pfft */
184 pfft_init();
185
186 /* Inititalize fftw */
187#ifdef USE_FFT_THREADS
188 fftw_init_threads();
189#endif
190 fftw_mpi_init();
191
192 if (set_fft_decomposition())
193 return 1;
194
195 if (!ThisTask)
196 dprintf(VMSG, ThisTask, "cube subdivision [%d dim]: %d x %d x %d = %d processes\n",
197 internal.tasks_subdivision_dim,
198 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0],
199 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1],
200 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2],
201 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0] *
202 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1] *
203 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2]);
204
205 if (pfft_create_procmesh(internal.tasks_subdivision_dim, MPI_COMM_WORLD, internal.tasks_subdivision_3D, &FFT_Comm))
206 {
207 int all = 1;
208 for (int iii = 0; iii < internal.tasks_subdivision_dim; iii++)
209 all *= internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[iii];
210
211 pfft_fprintf(MPI_COMM_WORLD, stderr, "Error while creating communicator and mesh with %d processes\n", all);
212 return 1;
213 }
214
215 return 0;
216}
217
218int set_parameters()
219{
220 int i;
221
222 /* set default internal parameters */
223 internal.verbose_level = VDBG;
224 internal.dump_seedplane = 0;
225 internal.dump_kdensity = 0;
226 internal.large_plane = 1;
227 internal.mimic_original_seedtable = 0;
228 // internal.dump_vectors = 0;
229 internal.constrain_task_decomposition[0] = 0;
230 internal.constrain_task_decomposition[1] = 0;
231 internal.constrain_task_decomposition[2] = 0;
232 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0] = 0;
233 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1] = 0;
234 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2] = 0;
235
236 if (read_parameter_file())
237 return 1;
238
239 /* the smallest legitimate value of MinHaloMass is 1 */
240 if (params.MinHaloMass <= 0)
241 params.MinHaloMass = 1;
242
243 if (params.BoxInH100)
244 {
245 params.BoxSize_h100 = params.BoxSize;
246 params.BoxSize_htrue = params.BoxSize / params.Hubble100;
247 }
248 else
249 {
250 params.BoxSize_h100 = params.BoxSize * params.Hubble100;
251 params.BoxSize_htrue = params.BoxSize;
252 }
253 params.InterPartDist = params.BoxSize_htrue / params.GridSize[0];
254
255 params.ParticleMass = 2.775499745e11 * params.Hubble100 * params.Hubble100 * params.Omega0 * pow(params.InterPartDist, 3.);
256 strcpy(params.DumpDir, "DumpProducts/");
257
258 /* The Nyquist wavenumber is used in generic calls of scale-dependent growth rates */
259 params.k_for_GM = PI / params.InterPartDist;
260
261 if (!params.NumFiles)
262 params.NumFiles = 1;
263
264 /* The number of files must be a divisor of the number of tasks */
265 if (NTasks % params.NumFiles != 0)
266 {
267 while (NTasks % params.NumFiles != 0)
268 params.NumFiles--;
269
270 if (!ThisTask)
271 printf("Warning: NumFiles must be a divisor of NTasks, it has been fixed to %d\n",
272 params.NumFiles);
273 }
274
275 /* inverse collapse times for the required outputs */
276 for (i = 0; i < outputs.n; i++)
277 outputs.F[i] = 1. + outputs.z[i];
278 outputs.Flast = outputs.F[outputs.n - 1];
279
280 if (!ThisTask)
281 {
282 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Flag for this run: %s\n\n", params.RunFlag);
283 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "PARAMETER VALUES from file %s:\n", params.ParameterFile);
284 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Omega0 %f\n", params.Omega0);
285 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "OmegaLambda %f\n", params.OmegaLambda);
286 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "OmegaBaryon %f\n", params.OmegaBaryon);
287 if (strcmp(params.TabulatedEoSfile, "no"))
288 {
289 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Dark Energy EoS will be read from file %s\n", params.TabulatedEoSfile);
290 }
291 else
292 {
293 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "DE EoS parameters %f %f\n", params.DEw0, params.DEwa);
294 }
295
296 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Hubble100 %f\n", params.Hubble100);
297 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Sigma8 %f\n", params.Sigma8);
298 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "PrimordialIndex %f\n", params.PrimordialIndex);
299 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "RandomSeed %d\n", params.RandomSeed);
300 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "PairedIC %d\n", params.PairedIC);
301 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "FixedIC %d\n", params.FixedIC);
302 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "OutputList %s\n", params.OutputList);
303 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Number of outputs %d\n", outputs.n);
304 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Output redshifts ");
305 for (i = 0; i < outputs.n; i++)
306 dprintf(VMSG, 0, " %f ", outputs.z[i]);
307 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "\n");
308 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "GridSize %d %d %d\n", params.GridSize[0], params.GridSize[1], params.GridSize[2]);
309 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "BoxSize (true Mpc) %f\n", params.BoxSize_htrue);
310 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "BoxSize (Mpc/h) %f\n", params.BoxSize_h100);
311 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Particle Mass (true Msun) %g\n", params.ParticleMass);
312 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Particle Mass (Msun/h) %g\n", params.ParticleMass * params.Hubble100);
313 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Inter-part dist (true Mpc) %f\n", params.InterPartDist);
314 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Inter-part dist (Mpc/h) %f\n", params.InterPartDist * params.Hubble100);
315 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "MinHaloMass (particles) %d\n", params.MinHaloMass);
316 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "MinHaloMass (Msun/h) %g\n", params.MinHaloMass * params.ParticleMass * params.Hubble100);
317 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "BoundaryLayerFactor %f\n", params.BoundaryLayerFactor);
318 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "MaxMem per task (Mb) %d\n", params.MaxMem);
319 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "MaxMem per particle (b) %f\n", params.MaxMemPerParticle);
320 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "PredPeakFactor %f\n", params.PredPeakFactor);
321 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "CatalogInAscii %d\n", params.CatalogInAscii);
322 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "NumFiles %d\n", params.NumFiles);
323 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "DoNotWriteCatalogs %d\n", params.DoNotWriteCatalogs);
324 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "DoNotWriteHistories %d\n", params.DoNotWriteHistories);
325 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "WriteTimelessSnapshot %d\n", params.WriteTimelessSnapshot);
326 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "OutputInH100 %d\n", params.OutputInH100);
327 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "DumpProducts %d\n", params.DumpProducts);
328 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "ReadProductsFromDumps %d\n", params.ReadProductsFromDumps);
329 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "ExitIfExtraParticles %d\n", params.ExitIfExtraParticles);
330
331 switch (params.AnalyticMassFunction)
332 {
333 case 0:
334 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Press & Schechter (1974) for the analytic mass function\n");
335 break;
336 case 1:
337 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Sheth & Tormen (2001) for the analytic mass function\n");
338 break;
339 case 2:
340 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Jenkins et al. (2001) for the analytic mass function\n");
341 break;
342 case 3:
343 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Warren et al. (2006) for the analytic mass function\n");
344 break;
345 case 4:
346 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Reed et al. (2007) for the analytic mass function\n");
347 break;
348 case 5:
349 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Crocce et al. (2010) for the analytic mass function\n");
350 break;
351 case 6:
352 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Tinker et al. (2008) for the analytic mass function\n");
353 break;
354 case 7:
355 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Courtin et al. (2010) for the analytic mass function\n");
356 break;
357 case 8:
358 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Angulo et al. (2012) for the analytic mass function\n");
359 break;
360 case 9:
361 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Watson et al. (2013) for the analytic mass function\n");
362 break;
363 case 10:
364 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Using Crocce et al. (2010) with forced universality for the analytic mass function\n");
365 break;
366 default:
367 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Unknown value for AnalyticMassFunction, Using Watson et al. (2013)\n");
368 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 9;
369 break;
370 }
371 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "\n");
372
373 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "\n");
374 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "GENIC parameters:\n");
375 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "InputSpectrum_UnitLength_in_cm %f\n", params.InputSpectrum_UnitLength_in_cm);
376 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "FileWithInputSpectrum %s\n", params.FileWithInputSpectrum);
377 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "WDM_PartMass_in_kev %f\n", params.WDM_PartMass_in_kev);
378#ifdef TABULATED_CT
379 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "CTtableFile %s\n", params.CTtableFile);
380#endif
381#ifdef READ_PK_TABLE
382 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "CAMBMatterFile %s\n", params.camb.MatterFile);
383 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "CAMBRedshiftsFile %s\n", params.camb.RedshiftsFile);
384#endif
385 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "\n");
386 }
387
388 /* Task 0 may have changed the value of this parameter */
389 MPI_Bcast(&params.AnalyticMassFunction, 1, MPI_INT, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
390
391 return 0;
392}
393
394int set_smoothing()
395{
396 int ismooth;
397 double var_min, var_max, rmin;
398
399 var_min = pow(1.686 / NSIGMA / GrowingMode(outputs.zlast, params.k_for_GM), 2.0);
400 rmin = params.InterPartDist / 6.;
401 var_max = MassVariance(rmin);
402 Smoothing.Nsmooth = (log10(var_max) - log10(var_min)) / STEP_VAR + 2;
403
404 if (Smoothing.Nsmooth <= 0)
405 {
406 if (!ThisTask)
407 dprintf(VERR, 0, "I am afraid that nothing is predicted to collapse in this configuration.\nI will work with no smoothing\n");
408 Smoothing.Nsmooth = 1;
409 }
410
411 if (!ThisTask)
412 {
413 printf("\nSMOOTHING RADII\n");
414 printf("Min variance: %f12.6, max variance: %f12.6, number of smoothing radii: %d\n",
415 var_min, var_max, Smoothing.Nsmooth);
416 }
417 Smoothing.Radius = (double *)malloc(Smoothing.Nsmooth * sizeof(double));
418 Smoothing.Variance = (double *)malloc(Smoothing.Nsmooth * sizeof(double));
419 Smoothing.TrueVariance = (double *)malloc(Smoothing.Nsmooth * sizeof(double));
420 if (Smoothing.Radius == 0x0 || Smoothing.Variance == 0x0 || Smoothing.TrueVariance == 0x0)
421 {
422 printf("ERROR on task %d: allocation of Smoothing failed\n", ThisTask);
423 fflush(stdout);
424 return 1;
425 }
426
427 for (ismooth = 0; ismooth < Smoothing.Nsmooth - 1; ismooth++)
428 {
429 Smoothing.Variance[ismooth] = pow(10., log10(var_min) + STEP_VAR * ismooth);
430 Smoothing.Radius[ismooth] = Radius(Smoothing.Variance[ismooth]);
431 }
432 Smoothing.Radius[ismooth] = 0.0;
433 Smoothing.Variance[ismooth] = var_max;
434
435 if (!ThisTask)
436 for (ismooth = 0; ismooth < Smoothing.Nsmooth; ismooth++)
437 printf(" %2d) Radius=%10f, Variance=%10f\n", ismooth + 1, Smoothing.Radius[ismooth], Smoothing.Variance[ismooth]);
438
439 fflush(stdout);
440 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
441
442 return 0;
443}
444
445int generate_densities()
446{
447
448 cputime.dens = MPI_Wtime();
449
450 if (!ThisTask)
451 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "[%s] Generating density in Fourier space\n", fdate());
452
453#ifdef WHITENOISE
454
455 if (Ngrids > 1)
456 {
457 if (!ThisTask)
458 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "Sorry, this works only with a single grid\n");
459 return 1;
460 }
461
462 if (read_white_noise())
463 return 1;
464
465#else
466
467 int igrid;
468 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
469 if (GenIC_large(igrid))
470 return 1;
471
472#endif
473
474 cputime.dens = MPI_Wtime() - cputime.dens;
475 if (!ThisTask)
476 dprintf(VMSG, 0, "[%s] Done generating density in Fourier space, cputime = %f s\n", fdate(), cputime.dens);
477
478 return 0;
479}
480
481int set_grids()
482{
483 /* initialization of fftw quantities on grids (one for the moment) */
484
485 int igrid, dim;
486
487 Ngrids = 1;
488
489 MyGrids = (grid_data *)malloc(Ngrids * sizeof(grid_data));
490
491 for (dim = 0; dim < 3; dim++)
492 MyGrids[0].GSglobal[dim] = params.GridSize[dim];
493
494 MyGrids[0].Ntotal = (unsigned long long)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_] *
495 (unsigned long long)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_] *
496 (unsigned long long)MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_];
497
498 MyGrids[0].BoxSize = params.BoxSize_htrue;
499 MyGrids[0].lower_k_cutoff = 0.;
500 MyGrids[0].upper_k_cutoff = NYQUIST * PI;
501
502 /* allocates pointers */
503 cvector_fft = (pfft_complex **)malloc(Ngrids * sizeof(fftw_complex *));
504 rvector_fft = (double **)malloc(Ngrids * sizeof(double *));
505
506 kdensity = (double **)malloc(Ngrids * sizeof(double *));
507 density = (double **)malloc(Ngrids * sizeof(double *));
508 first_derivatives = (double ***)malloc(Ngrids * sizeof(double **));
509 second_derivatives = (double ***)malloc(Ngrids * sizeof(double **));
510
511 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
512 {
513 first_derivatives[igrid] = (double **)malloc(3 * sizeof(double *));
514 second_derivatives[igrid] = (double **)malloc(6 * sizeof(double *));
515 }
516 /* moved to GenIC */
517 /* seedtable=(unsigned int**)malloc(Ngrids * sizeof(unsigned int*)); */
518
519 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
520 if (set_one_grid(igrid))
521 return 1;
522
523 /* Task 0 broadcasts its number of fft particles, that becomes the reference */
524 unsigned int PPT;
525 if (!ThisTask)
526 PPT = MyGrids[0].total_local_size;
527
528 MPI_Bcast(&PPT, 1, MPI_UNSIGNED, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
529 MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask = PPT;
530
531 if ((int)(PPT * params.MaxMemPerParticle / MBYTE + 1.0) > params.MaxMem)
532 {
533 if (!ThisTask)
534 {
535 printf("ERROR: MaxMem of %d Mb per task is insufficient to store %d bytes per particle\n",
536 params.MaxMem, (int)params.MaxMemPerParticle);
537 printf(" please increase MaxMem to at least %d\n", (int)(PPT * params.MaxMemPerParticle / MBYTE + 1.0));
538 }
539 return 1;
540 }
541
542 return 0;
543}
544
545#ifdef PLC
546#define NSAFE 2.0
547
548int cone_and_cube_intersect(double *, double *, double *, double *, double, double *, double *, int *);
549double maxF(double *, double *, double *, double *, double *);
550
551int set_plc(void)
552{
553 int NAll, ir, jr, kr, ic, this, intersection, axis;
554 double Largest_r, Smallest_r, smallestr, largestr, x[3], l[3], z, d, mod, displ_variance, tdis;
555 FILE *fout;
556 char filename[LBLENGTH];
557
558 /* ordering of coordinates to accomodate for rotation caused by fft ordering */
559 static int rot[3] = {0, 1, 2};
560
561 if (params.StartingzForPLC < 0.)
562 {
563 plc.Nreplications = 0;
564 plc.Fstart = plc.Fstop = -1.;
565 plc.Nmax = 0;
566 plc.Nexpected = 0;
567 if (!ThisTask)
568 printf("Negative value of StartingzForPLC, no Past Light Cone output will be given\n\n");
569
570 return 0;
571 }
572
573 /* Ensure the PLC redshift interval is ordered as Startingz >= Lastz
574 to avoid negative bin counts and inconsistencies downstream. */
575 if (params.StartingzForPLC < params.LastzForPLC)
576 {
577 if (!ThisTask)
578 printf("Warning: StartingzForPLC (%g) < LastzForPLC (%g). Swapping them to ensure a valid PLC interval.\n",
579 params.StartingzForPLC, params.LastzForPLC);
580 double tmpz = params.StartingzForPLC;
581 params.StartingzForPLC = params.LastzForPLC;
582 params.LastzForPLC = tmpz;
583 }
584
585 /* here we define the vertex and axis direction of the cone */
586 if (params.PLCProvideConeData)
587 {
588 /* in this case data are provided in the parameter file */
589 plc.center[rot[0]] = params.PLCCenter[0] / params.BoxSize * params.GridSize[0];
590 plc.center[rot[1]] = params.PLCCenter[1] / params.BoxSize * params.GridSize[0];
591 plc.center[rot[2]] = params.PLCCenter[2] / params.BoxSize * params.GridSize[0];
592 plc.zvers[rot[0]] = params.PLCAxis[0];
593 plc.zvers[rot[1]] = params.PLCAxis[1];
594 plc.zvers[rot[2]] = params.PLCAxis[2];
595 }
596 else
597 {
598 /* in this case the center is randomly placed and the direction points toward the main diagonal */
599 gsl_rng_set(random_generator, params.RandomSeed);
600 plc.center[0] = gsl_rng_uniform(random_generator) * MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_];
601 plc.center[1] = gsl_rng_uniform(random_generator) * MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_];
602 plc.center[2] = gsl_rng_uniform(random_generator) * MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_];
603 double mytheta = acos(2 * gsl_rng_uniform(random_generator) - 1);
604 double myphi = gsl_rng_uniform(random_generator) * 2.0 * PI;
605 plc.zvers[0] = sin(mytheta) * cos(myphi);
606 plc.zvers[1] = sin(mytheta) * sin(myphi);
607 plc.zvers[2] = cos(mytheta);
608 }
609
610 /* normalization of the cone axis direction */
611 mod = sqrt(plc.zvers[0] * plc.zvers[0] + plc.zvers[1] * plc.zvers[1] + plc.zvers[2] * plc.zvers[2]);
612 for (ir = 0; ir < 3; ir++)
613 plc.zvers[ir] /= mod;
614
615 /* here we define a system where zvers is the z-axis */
616 if (plc.zvers[2] == 1.0)
617 {
618 /* if the zversor corresponds to the z axis use the existing axes */
619 plc.xvers[0] = 1.0;
620 plc.xvers[1] = 0.0;
621 plc.xvers[2] = 0.0;
622 plc.yvers[0] = 0.0;
623 plc.yvers[1] = 1.0;
624 plc.yvers[2] = 0.0;
625 }
626 else
627 {
628 /* x axis will be oriented as the cross product of zvers and the z axis */
629 mod = sqrt(plc.zvers[0] * plc.zvers[0] + plc.zvers[1] * plc.zvers[1]);
630 plc.xvers[0] = plc.zvers[1] / mod;
631 plc.xvers[1] = -plc.zvers[0] / mod;
632 plc.xvers[2] = 0.0;
633 /* y axis will be the cross product of z and x */
634 plc.yvers[0] = plc.zvers[1] * plc.xvers[2] - plc.zvers[2] * plc.xvers[1];
635 plc.yvers[1] = plc.zvers[2] * plc.xvers[0] - plc.zvers[0] * plc.xvers[2];
636 plc.yvers[2] = plc.zvers[0] * plc.xvers[1] - plc.zvers[1] * plc.xvers[0];
637 }
638
639 /* initialization to compute the number of realizations */
640 NAll = (int)(ComovingDistance(params.StartingzForPLC) / MyGrids[0].BoxSize) + 2;
641 plc.Fstart = 1. + params.StartingzForPLC;
642 plc.Fstop = 1. + params.LastzForPLC;
643
644 Largest_r = ComovingDistance(params.StartingzForPLC) / params.InterPartDist;
645 Smallest_r = ComovingDistance(params.LastzForPLC) / params.InterPartDist;
646
647 /* this is needed to compute the typical displacement */
648 displ_variance = sqrt(DisplVariance(params.InterPartDist)) / params.InterPartDist;
649 Smallest_r -= NSAFE * GrowingMode(params.LastzForPLC, params.k_for_GM) * displ_variance;
650 Smallest_r = (Smallest_r > 0 ? Smallest_r : 0.);
651 Largest_r += NSAFE * GrowingMode(params.StartingzForPLC, params.k_for_GM) * displ_variance;
652
653 l[0] = (double)(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_]);
654 l[1] = (double)(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_]);
655 l[2] = (double)(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_]);
656
657 /* first, it counts the number of replications needed */
658 plc.Nreplications = 0;
659 for (ir = -NAll; ir <= NAll; ir++)
660 for (jr = -NAll; jr <= NAll; jr++)
661 for (kr = -NAll; kr <= NAll; kr++)
662 {
663 x[0] = ir * l[0];
664 x[1] = jr * l[1];
665 x[2] = kr * l[2];
666
667 intersection = cone_and_cube_intersect(x, l, plc.center, plc.zvers, params.PLCAperture, &smallestr, &largestr, &axis);
668
669 if (intersection && !(smallestr > Largest_r || largestr < Smallest_r))
670 plc.Nreplications++;
671 }
672
673 /* second, it allocates the needed memory for the replications */
674 plc.repls = malloc(plc.Nreplications * sizeof(replication_data));
675
676 if (!ThisTask)
677 {
678 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%s.geometry.out", params.RunFlag);
679 fout = fopen(filename, "w");
680 fprintf(fout, "# N. replications: %d (out of %d checked)\n", plc.Nreplications, (2 * NAll + 1) * (2 * NAll + 1) * (2 * NAll + 1));
681 fprintf(fout, "# distance range: %10.6f %10.6f\n", Smallest_r, Largest_r);
682 fprintf(fout, "# V = %10.6f %10.6f %10.6f\n", plc.center[0], plc.center[1], plc.center[2]);
683 fprintf(fout, "# D = %10.6f %10.6f %10.6f\n", plc.zvers[0], plc.zvers[1], plc.zvers[2]);
684 fprintf(fout, "# L = %10.6f %10.6f %10.6f\n", l[0], l[1], l[2]);
685 fprintf(fout, "# A = %10.6f\n", params.PLCAperture);
686 fprintf(fout, "# IPD = %10.6f\n", params.InterPartDist);
687 fprintf(fout, "#\n");
688 }
689
690 /* third, it stores information on replications */
691 this = 0;
692 for (ir = -NAll; ir <= NAll; ir++)
693 for (jr = -NAll; jr <= NAll; jr++)
694 for (kr = -NAll; kr <= NAll; kr++)
695 {
696 x[0] = ir * l[0];
697 x[1] = jr * l[1];
698 x[2] = kr * l[2];
699
700 intersection = cone_and_cube_intersect(x, l, plc.center, plc.zvers, params.PLCAperture, &smallestr, &largestr, &axis);
701
702 if (intersection && !(smallestr > Largest_r || largestr < Smallest_r))
703 {
704 if (!ThisTask)
705 fprintf(fout, " %3d %3d %3d %3d %10.6f %10.6f %d %d\n", this, ir, jr, kr, smallestr, largestr, intersection, axis);
706 plc.repls[this].i = ir;
707 plc.repls[this].j = jr;
708 plc.repls[this].k = kr;
709 plc.repls[this].F1 = -largestr;
710 plc.repls[this++].F2 = -smallestr;
711 }
712 }
713 if (!ThisTask)
714 fclose(fout);
715
716 /* fourth it transforms distances to redshifts */
717 for (z = 100.; z >= 0.0; z -= 0.01)
718 {
719 /* NSAFE times the typical displacement at z */
720 tdis = NSAFE * GrowingMode(z, params.k_for_GM) * displ_variance;
721
722 /* comoving distance at redshift z */
723 d = ComovingDistance(z) / params.InterPartDist;
724 for (this = 0; this < plc.Nreplications; this++)
725 {
726 if (plc.repls[this].F1 <= 0.0 && d < -plc.repls[this].F1 + tdis)
727 plc.repls[this].F1 = z + 0.01 + 1.0;
728 if (plc.repls[this].F2 <= 0.0 && d < -plc.repls[this].F2 - tdis)
729 plc.repls[this].F2 = z + 1.0;
730 }
731 }
732 for (this = 0; this < plc.Nreplications; this++)
733 {
734 if (plc.repls[this].F1 <= 0.0)
735 plc.repls[this].F1 = 1.0;
736 if (plc.repls[this].F2 <= 0.0)
737 plc.repls[this].F2 = 1.0;
738 }
739
740 plc.Nmax = (int)(MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask / 6 * params.PredPeakFactor);
741
742 /* n(z) for the light cone */
743 plc.delta_z = 0.05; /* NB: this is hard-coded... */
744 plc.nzbins = (int)((params.StartingzForPLC - params.LastzForPLC) / plc.delta_z + 0.1);
745 if (plc.nzbins < 0)
746 {
747 /* This should not happen due to the swap above, but guard anyway */
748 if (!ThisTask)
749 printf("ERROR: computed negative plc.nzbins=%d. Check PLC redshift settings. Forcing nzbins=0 to continue safely.\n",
750 plc.nzbins);
751 plc.nzbins = 0;
752 }
753 plc.nz = (double *)calloc(plc.nzbins, sizeof(double));
754
755 if (!ThisTask)
756 {
757 printf("\nThe Past Light Cone will be reconstructed from z=%f to z=%f\n",
758 params.StartingzForPLC, params.LastzForPLC);
759 if (params.PLCProvideConeData)
760 printf("Cone data have been provided in the parameter file\n");
761 else
762 printf("Cone data have been decided by the code\n");
763 printf("Past Light Cone will be centred on point [%f,%f,%f] (true Mpc)\n",
764 plc.center[0] * params.InterPartDist, plc.center[1] * params.InterPartDist, plc.center[2] * params.InterPartDist);
765 printf("The cone vertex will be pointed toward [%f,%f,%f]\n", plc.zvers[0], plc.zvers[1], plc.zvers[2]);
766 printf("It will have an aperture of %f degrees\n", params.PLCAperture);
767 printf("The comoving distance at the starting redshift, z=%f, is: %f Mpc\n",
768 params.StartingzForPLC, Largest_r * params.InterPartDist);
769 printf("The comoving distance at the stopping redshift, z=%f, is: %f Mpc\n",
770 params.LastzForPLC, Smallest_r * params.InterPartDist);
771 printf("The reconstruction will be done for %f < z < %f\n", params.LastzForPLC, params.StartingzForPLC);
772 printf("The corresponding F values are: Fstart=%f, Fstop=%f\n", plc.Fstart, plc.Fstop);
773 printf("The box will be replicated %d times to construct the PLC\n", plc.Nreplications);
774 for (ic = 0; ic < plc.Nreplications; ic++)
775 printf(" Replication %2d: shift (%2d,%2d,%2d), from F=%f to F=%f\n",
776 ic, plc.repls[ic].i, plc.repls[ic].j, plc.repls[ic].k,
777 plc.repls[ic].F1, plc.repls[ic].F2);
778 printf("Task 0 will use plc.Nmax=%d\n", plc.Nmax);
779 printf("The halo number density will be output in %d redshift bins\n", plc.nzbins);
780 printf("\n");
781 }
782
783 return 0;
784}
785
786double maxF(double *P, double *V, double *U, double *D, double *L)
787{
788 /* determines the smallest angle between a segment and a cone direction;
789 the cone vertex is in a point vec(V), its direction is versor(D);
790 the segment starts from point vec(P) and goes along the direction versor(U), for a length L;
791 the code returns the cos of the smallest angle between the cone axis and
792 the line that joins vec(V) with a point of the segment.
793 */
794
795 double dP = sqrt((P[0] - V[0]) * (P[0] - V[0]) + (P[1] - V[1]) * (P[1] - V[1]) + (P[2] - V[2]) * (P[2] - V[2]));
796 if (dP == 0.0)
797 return 1.0;
798 double cosDU = (D[0] * U[0] + D[1] * U[1] + D[2] * U[2]);
799 double cosDP = (D[0] * (P[0] - V[0]) + D[1] * (P[1] - V[1]) + D[2] * (P[2] - V[2])) / dP;
800 double cosUP = (U[0] * (P[0] - V[0]) + U[1] * (P[1] - V[1]) + U[2] * (P[2] - V[2])) / dP;
801
802 if (cosDP - cosDU * cosUP == 0.0)
803 return 0.0;
804 double tmax = (cosDU - cosDP * cosUP) / (cosDP - cosDU * cosUP);
805 if (tmax < 0)
806 tmax = 0.0;
807 else if (tmax > *L / dP)
808 tmax = *L / dP;
809
810 return (cosDP + tmax * cosDU) / sqrt(1.0 + tmax * tmax + 2. * tmax * cosUP);
811}
812
813int cone_and_cube_intersect(double *Oc, double *L, double *V, double *D, double theta, double *rmin, double *rmax, int *axis)
814{
815 int i, j, k, ivec[3], dim, dim1, dim2;
816 double r, x, F, Fmax, costh, proj, U[3], P[3];
817
818 /* This routine returns >=1 if the cone with vertex V, axis
819 direction D and semi-aperture theta (deg) intersects the cube
820 (parallelepiped) starting from point Oc and with edges of lenght
821 L aligned with the axes. It returns 0 if the two solids do not
822 intersect. It also computes the smallest and largest distances
823 of the cube from the cone vertex V. The bits of the integer axis
824 will encode the cube faces that are intersected by the cone axis.
825 */
826
827 /* Initialization of rmin and rmax */
828 *rmin = 1.e32;
829 *rmax = 0.0;
830
831 /* max distance is computed using the cube vertices */
832 for (i = 0; i < 2; i++)
833 for (j = 0; j < 2; j++)
834 for (k = 0; k < 2; k++)
835 {
836 r = sqrt(pow(Oc[0] + i * L[0] - V[0], 2.0) +
837 pow(Oc[1] + j * L[1] - V[1], 2.0) +
838 pow(Oc[2] + k * L[2] - V[2], 2.0));
839 if (r > *rmax)
840 *rmax = r;
841 }
842
843 /* min distance from cube faces */
844 /* and intersection of axis with cube faces */
845 *axis = 0;
846 for (dim = 0; dim < 3; dim++) /* three dimension (normals to cube faces) */
847 for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) /* two faces per dimension */
848 {
849 proj = Oc[dim] - V[dim] + i * L[dim];
850 dim1 = (dim + 1) % 3;
851 dim2 = (dim + 2) % 3;
852
853 /* minimum distance */
854 r = proj * proj; /* the normal component always contributes */
855 if (V[dim1] < Oc[dim1]) /* only of their projection is outside the face */
856 r += pow(V[dim1] - Oc[dim1], 2.0);
857 else if (V[dim1] >= Oc[dim1] + L[dim1])
858 r += pow(V[dim1] - Oc[dim1] - L[dim1], 2.0);
859 if (V[dim2] < Oc[dim2])
860 r += pow(V[dim2] - Oc[dim2], 2.0);
861 else if (V[dim2] >= Oc[dim2] + L[dim2])
862 r += pow(V[dim2] - Oc[dim2] - L[dim2], 2.0);
863 r = sqrt(r);
864 if (r < *rmin)
865 *rmin = r;
866
867 /* axis intersection */
868 if ((x = proj / D[dim]) > 0.0 &&
869 V[dim1] + x * D[dim1] >= Oc[dim1] &&
870 V[dim1] + x * D[dim1] < Oc[dim1] + L[dim1] &&
871 V[dim2] + x * D[dim2] >= Oc[dim2] &&
872 V[dim2] + x * D[dim2] < Oc[dim2] + L[dim2])
873 *axis += 1 << (dim + i * 3);
874 }
875
876 /* step 1: if the vertex V is inside the cube then they intersect */
877 if ((V[0] >= Oc[0] && V[0] < Oc[0] + L[0] &&
878 V[1] >= Oc[1] && V[1] < Oc[1] + L[1] &&
879 V[2] >= Oc[2] && V[2] < Oc[2] + L[2]))
880 {
881 *rmin = 0.0; /* in this case rmin is unrelated to the cube boundary */
882 return 1;
883 }
884
885 /* step 2: if the whole sky is required, only rmin and rmax are needed */
886 if (theta >= 180.)
887 return 2;
888
889 /* step 3: if the axis intersects one face then there is an intersection */
890 if (*axis)
891 return 3;
892
893 /* step4: compute maximum of ** F = (P-V) dot D /|P-V| - cos theta **
894 for each cube edge */
895 Fmax = -10.0;
896 costh = cos(theta / 180. * PI);
897 for (i = 0; i < 2; i++)
898 for (j = 0; j < 2; j++)
899 for (k = 0; k < 2; k++)
900 {
901 ivec[0] = i;
902 ivec[1] = j;
903 ivec[2] = k;
904
905 for (dim = 0; dim < 3; dim++)
906 if (!ivec[dim])
907 {
908 U[dim] = 1.0;
909 U[(dim + 1) % 3] = 0.0;
910 U[(dim + 2) % 3] = 0.0;
911 P[0] = Oc[0] + ivec[0] * L[0];
912 P[1] = Oc[1] + ivec[1] * L[1];
913 P[2] = Oc[2] + ivec[2] * L[2];
914 F = maxF(P, V, U, D, L + dim) - costh;
915 if (F > Fmax)
916 Fmax = F;
917 }
918 }
919
920 /* if the nearest vertex is inside the cone then exit */
921 if (Fmax > 0)
922 return 4;
923
924 /* at this point the cone and the cube do not intersect */
925 return 0;
926}
927
928#else
929
930int set_plc()
931{
932 if (!ThisTask)
933 printf("PLC flag at compilation was not set, no Past Light Cone output will be given\n\n");
934
935 return 0;
936}
937
938#endif
939
940/* division in sub-boxes */
941int set_subboxes()
942{
943
944 int i, j, k, i1 = 0, j1 = 0, k1 = 0, NN, NN1, N1, N2, N3;
945 unsigned long long int surface, this, tt;
946 double size, sizeG, cc;
947
948 /* mass of the largest halo expected in the box */
949 params.Largest = 1.e18;
950 cc = 1. / pow(params.BoxSize_htrue, 3.0);
951 double aa = AnalyticMassFunction(params.Largest, outputs.zlast);
952 while (aa * params.Largest < cc)
953 {
954 params.Largest *= 0.99;
955 aa = AnalyticMassFunction(params.Largest, outputs.zlast);
956 }
957 size = SizeForMass(params.Largest);
958 sizeG = size / params.InterPartDist;
959
960 /*
961 The number of loadable subbox particles is equal
962 to the number allowed by the specified MaxMemPerParticle.
963 The boundary layer is set to its maximum value.
964 */
965
966 /* finds the optimal number of sub-boxes to use for the fragmentation */
967
968 surface = MyGrids[0].Ntotal;
969 for (k = 1; k <= NTasks; k++)
970 for (j = 1; j <= NTasks / k; j++)
971 for (i = 1; i <= NTasks / k / j; i++)
972 /* the three indices must be exact divisors of the three grid lengths */
973 if (i * j * k == NTasks)
974 {
975 /* number of particles in the sub-box */
976 N1 = find_length(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_], i, 0);
977 N2 = find_length(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_], j, 0);
978 N3 = find_length(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_], k, 0);
979
980 this = (unsigned long long int)(i > 1 ? 2 * (N2 * N3) : 0) +
981 (unsigned long long int)(j > 1 ? 2 * (N1 * N3) : 0) +
982 (unsigned long long int)(k > 1 ? 2 * (N1 * N2) : 0);
983 tt = this;
984 if (N1 / 2 < sizeG)
985 this += (unsigned long long int)((double)tt * pow(2 * sizeG / (double)N1, 2.0));
986 if (N2 / 2 < sizeG)
987 this += (unsigned long long int)((double)tt * pow(2 * sizeG / (double)N2, 2.0));
988 if (N3 / 2 < sizeG)
989 this += (unsigned long long int)((double)tt * pow(2 * sizeG / (double)N3, 2.0));
990
991 if (this < surface)
992 {
993 surface = this;
994 i1 = i;
995 j1 = j;
996 k1 = k;
997 }
998 }
999
1000 subbox.nbox[_x_] = i1;
1001 subbox.nbox[_y_] = j1;
1002 subbox.nbox[_z_] = k1;
1003
1004 /* mybox is the box assigned to the task */
1005 NN = subbox.nbox[_y_] * subbox.nbox[_z_];
1006 if (NN == 0)
1007 {
1008 printf("ERROR: I could not find a valid subbox subdivision\n");
1009 printf(" subbox.nbox = [%d,%d,%d]\n", i1, j1, k1);
1010 printf(" please try again with a different number of tasks\n");
1011 return 1;
1012 }
1013
1014 subbox.mybox[_x_] = ThisTask / NN;
1015 NN1 = ThisTask - subbox.mybox[_x_] * NN;
1016 subbox.mybox[_y_] = NN1 / subbox.nbox[_z_];
1017 subbox.mybox[_z_] = NN1 - subbox.mybox[_y_] * subbox.nbox[_z_];
1018
1019 subbox.Lgrid[_x_] = find_length(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_], subbox.nbox[_x_], subbox.mybox[_x_]);
1020 subbox.Lgrid[_y_] = find_length(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_], subbox.nbox[_y_], subbox.mybox[_y_]);
1021 subbox.Lgrid[_z_] = find_length(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_], subbox.nbox[_z_], subbox.mybox[_z_]);
1022
1023 subbox.pbc[_x_] = (subbox.nbox[_x_] == 1);
1024 subbox.pbc[_y_] = (subbox.nbox[_y_] == 1);
1025 subbox.pbc[_z_] = (subbox.nbox[_z_] == 1);
1026
1027 /* #ifndef BL_GRANDISSIMA */
1028
1029 /* subbox.safe[_x_] = (subbox.pbc[_x_] ? 0 : (find_length(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_],subbox.nbox[_x_],0)-1)/2); */
1030 /* subbox.safe[_y_] = (subbox.pbc[_y_] ? 0 : (find_length(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_],subbox.nbox[_y_],0)-1)/2); */
1031 /* subbox.safe[_z_] = (subbox.pbc[_z_] ? 0 : (find_length(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_],subbox.nbox[_z_],0)-1)/2); */
1032
1033 /* #else */
1034
1035 /* the boundary layer can be as large as to nearly fill the whole box,
1036 but the number of particles must be represented by an unsigned int */
1037 int BB = (int)(params.BoundaryLayerFactor * sizeG + 1);
1038 subbox.safe[_x_] = (subbox.pbc[_x_] ? 0 : (BB > MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_] / 2 - subbox.Lgrid[_x_] / 2 - 1 ? MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_] / 2 - subbox.Lgrid[_x_] / 2 - 1 : BB));
1039 subbox.safe[_y_] = (subbox.pbc[_y_] ? 0 : (BB > MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_] / 2 - subbox.Lgrid[_y_] / 2 - 1 ? MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_] / 2 - subbox.Lgrid[_y_] / 2 - 1 : BB));
1040 subbox.safe[_z_] = (subbox.pbc[_z_] ? 0 : (BB > MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_] / 2 - subbox.Lgrid[_z_] / 2 - 1 ? MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_] / 2 - subbox.Lgrid[_z_] / 2 - 1 : BB));
1041
1042 // #endif
1043
1044 subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] = subbox.Lgrid[_x_] + 2 * subbox.safe[_x_];
1045 subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] = subbox.Lgrid[_y_] + 2 * subbox.safe[_y_];
1046 subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] = subbox.Lgrid[_z_] + 2 * subbox.safe[_z_];
1047 unsigned long long MySize = (long long)subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] * (long long)subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] * (long long)subbox.Lgwbl[_z_];
1048 while (MySize > (unsigned long long)1 << 31)
1049 {
1050 subbox.safe[_x_] -= 1;
1051 subbox.safe[_y_] -= 1;
1052 subbox.safe[_z_] -= 1;
1053 subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] = subbox.Lgrid[_x_] + 2 * subbox.safe[_x_];
1054 subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] = subbox.Lgrid[_y_] + 2 * subbox.safe[_y_];
1055 subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] = subbox.Lgrid[_z_] + 2 * subbox.safe[_z_];
1056 MySize = (unsigned long long)subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] * (unsigned long long)subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] * (unsigned long long)subbox.Lgwbl[_z_];
1057 }
1058
1059 subbox.start[_x_] = find_start(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_], subbox.nbox[_x_], subbox.mybox[_x_]);
1060 subbox.start[_y_] = find_start(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_], subbox.nbox[_y_], subbox.mybox[_y_]);
1061 subbox.start[_z_] = find_start(MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_], subbox.nbox[_z_], subbox.mybox[_z_]);
1062
1063 subbox.stabl[_x_] = subbox.start[_x_] - subbox.safe[_x_];
1064 subbox.stabl[_y_] = subbox.start[_y_] - subbox.safe[_y_];
1065 subbox.stabl[_z_] = subbox.start[_z_] - subbox.safe[_z_];
1066
1067 /*
1068 Npart: total number of particles in the whole sub-volume
1069 Ngood: total number of particles in the well reconstructed region
1070 Npredpeaks: a guess of the maximum number of peaks in the subbox
1071 Nalloc: number of particles for which memory has been allocated (set in organize_main_memory)
1072 Nstored: number of actually stored particles
1073 */
1074
1075 subbox.Npart = subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] * subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] * subbox.Lgwbl[_z_];
1076 subbox.Ngood = subbox.Lgrid[_x_] * subbox.Lgrid[_y_] * subbox.Lgrid[_z_];
1077 /* this is a prediction of the number of peaks that will be found */
1078 subbox.PredNpeaks = (int)(MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask / 6 * params.PredPeakFactor);
1079 subbox.Nstored = 0;
1080 /* this is the size of frag_map*/
1081 subbox.maplength = subbox.Npart / UINTLEN + (subbox.Npart % UINTLEN != 0);
1082 if ((subbox.Nalloc = organize_main_memory()) == 0)
1083 {
1084 fflush(stdout);
1085 if (!ThisTask)
1086 printf("organize_main_memory returned an invalid Nalloc, exiting\n");
1087 return 1;
1088 }
1089
1090 /* messagges */
1091 if (!ThisTask)
1092 {
1093 printf("\n");
1094 printf("FRAGMENTATION:\n");
1095 printf("Reference number of particles: %d\n", MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask);
1096 printf("Requested bytes per particle: %d\n", (int)params.MaxMemPerParticle);
1097 printf("Number of sub-boxes per dimension: %d %d %d\n", subbox.nbox[_x_], subbox.nbox[_y_], subbox.nbox[_z_]);
1098 printf("Periodic boundary conditions: %d %d %d\n", subbox.pbc[_x_], subbox.pbc[_y_], subbox.pbc[_z_]);
1099 printf("Core 0 will work on a grid: %d %d %d\n", subbox.Lgwbl[_x_], subbox.Lgwbl[_y_], subbox.Lgwbl[_z_]);
1100 printf("The resolved box will be: %d %d %d\n", subbox.Lgrid[_x_], subbox.Lgrid[_y_], subbox.Lgrid[_z_]);
1101 printf("Boundary layer: %d %d %d\n", subbox.safe[_x_], subbox.safe[_y_], subbox.safe[_z_]);
1102 printf("Boundary layer factor: %f\n", params.BoundaryLayerFactor);
1103 printf("Number of total particles for core 0: %d\n", subbox.Npart);
1104 printf("Number of good particles for core 0: %d\n", subbox.Ngood);
1105 printf("Particles that core 0 will allocate: %d\n", subbox.Nalloc);
1106 printf("Allowed overhead for boundary layer: %f\n", (float)subbox.Nalloc / (float)MyGrids[0].ParticlesPerTask);
1107 printf("Largest halo expected in this box at z=%f: %e Msun\n",
1108 outputs.zlast, params.Largest);
1109 printf(" its Lagrangian size: %f Mpc (%6.2f grid points)\n", size, sizeG);
1110 printf(" this requires a boundary layer of %6.2f grid points \n", sizeG * params.BoundaryLayerFactor);
1111
1112#ifndef BL_GRANDISSIMA
1113 if ((!subbox.pbc[_x_] && params.BoundaryLayerFactor * sizeG > subbox.safe[_x_]) ||
1114 (!subbox.pbc[_y_] && params.BoundaryLayerFactor * sizeG > subbox.safe[_y_]) ||
1115 (!subbox.pbc[_z_] && params.BoundaryLayerFactor * sizeG > subbox.safe[_z_]))
1116 {
1117 printf("WARNING: the boundary layer on some dimension is smaller than the predicted size of the largest halos\n");
1118 printf(" times the BoundaryLayerFactor, the most massive halos may be inaccurate\n");
1119 }
1120#endif
1121 }
1122
1123 /* initialization of quantities required by compute_mf */
1124 if (params.OutputInH100)
1125 mf.hfactor = params.Hubble100;
1126 else
1127 mf.hfactor = 1.0;
1128 mf.hfactor4 = pow(mf.hfactor, 4.);
1129 mf.vol = (double)MyGrids[0].Ntotal * pow(params.InterPartDist, 3.0);
1130 mf.mmin = log10(params.MinHaloMass * params.ParticleMass) - 0.001 * DELTAM;
1131 mf.mmax = log10(params.Largest) + 3.0 * DELTAM;
1132 mf.NBIN = (int)((mf.mmax - mf.mmin) / DELTAM) + 1;
1133 mf.ninbin = (int *)calloc(mf.NBIN, sizeof(int));
1134 mf.ninbin_local = (int *)calloc(mf.NBIN, sizeof(int));
1135 mf.massinbin = (double *)calloc(mf.NBIN, sizeof(double));
1136 mf.massinbin_local = (double *)calloc(mf.NBIN, sizeof(double));
1137
1138 /* messages */
1139 if (!ThisTask)
1140 {
1141 printf("\nThe mass function will be computed from Log M=%f to Log M=%f (%d bins)\n",
1142 mf.mmin, mf.mmax, mf.NBIN);
1143 printf("\n");
1144 fflush(stdout);
1145 }
1146
1147 return 0;
1148}
1149
1150int find_start(int L, int n, int ibox)
1151{
1152 int LL, MM;
1153
1154 if (n == 1)
1155 return 0;
1156 else
1157 {
1158 LL = L / n;
1159 MM = L % n;
1160 if (ibox == 0)
1161 return 0;
1162 else if (ibox <= MM)
1163 return ibox * (LL + 1);
1164 else
1165 return ibox * LL + MM;
1166 }
1167}
1168
1169int find_length(int L, int n, int ibox)
1170{
1171 /* finds the length of a subbox, given the grid length L,
1172 the number of subboxes n and the subbox id ibox */
1173
1174 int LL, MM;
1175
1176 if (n == 1)
1177 return L;
1178 else
1179 {
1180 LL = L / n;
1181 MM = L % n;
1182 if (ibox < MM)
1183 return LL + 1;
1184 else
1185 return LL;
1186 }
1187}
1188unsigned int gcd(unsigned int u, unsigned int v)
1189// this version of greatest common divisor taken
1190// from Daniel Lemire's blog
1191// lemire.me/blog/2013/12/26/fastest-way-to-compute-the-greatest-common-divisor/
1192{
1193 if (u == 0)
1194 return v;
1195 if (v == 0)
1196 return u;
1197 int shift = __builtin_ctz(u | v);
1198 u >>= __builtin_ctz(u);
1199 do
1200 {
1201 v >>= __builtin_ctz(v);
1202 if (u > v)
1203 {
1204 unsigned int t = v;
1205 v = u;
1206 u = t;
1207 }
1208 v = v - u;
1209 } while (v != 0);
1210 return u << shift;
1211}
1212
1213int set_fft_decomposition(void)
1214{
1215
1216 /* initialize task mesh for pfft */
1217 /* it's up to you to decide HOW to subdivide work in 3D, and then to store it in task_subdivision_3D*/
1218
1219 int decomposition_done = 0;
1220
1221 if (internal.constrain_task_decomposition[0] +
1222 internal.constrain_task_decomposition[1] +
1223 internal.constrain_task_decomposition[2] >
1224 0)
1225
1226 // some constraints about how to decompose fft are set in parameter file
1227 {
1228 // --- check trivial errors
1229 // just ot be sure about trivial typos, check that none is < 0
1230 if (internal.constrain_task_decomposition[0] < 0 ||
1231 internal.constrain_task_decomposition[1] < 0 ||
1232 internal.constrain_task_decomposition[2] < 0)
1233 {
1234 dprintf(VXERR, 0, "you can't constraint FFt decomposition with negative values\n");
1235 return 1;
1236 }
1237
1238 if (internal.constrain_task_decomposition[0] == 0)
1239 {
1240 dprintf(VXERR, 0, "you can't constraint FFt decomposition leaving first dimension to 0\n");
1241 return 1;
1242 }
1243 // -------------------------
1244
1245 // set first dimension
1246 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0] = internal.constrain_task_decomposition[0];
1247 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1] = internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2] = 1;
1248 decomposition_done = 1;
1249
1250 if (internal.constrain_task_decomposition[0] == NTasks)
1251 {
1252 // all tasks are in dimension 1
1253 internal.tasks_subdivision_dim = 1;
1254 decomposition_done = 3;
1255 }
1256
1257 else if (internal.constrain_task_decomposition[1] > 0)
1258 {
1259 // --- check trivial errors
1260 if (internal.constrain_task_decomposition[0] * internal.constrain_task_decomposition[1] > NTasks)
1261 {
1262 dprintf(VXERR, 0, "you specified a wrong fft decomposition: Dim0 x Dim1 = %d > %d tasks\n",
1263 internal.constrain_task_decomposition[0] * internal.constrain_task_decomposition[1], NTasks);
1264 return 1;
1265 }
1266 // ------------------------
1267
1268 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1] = internal.constrain_task_decomposition[1];
1269
1270 if (internal.constrain_task_decomposition[0] * internal.constrain_task_decomposition[1] == NTasks)
1271 {
1272 // all tasks are in dimension 1 and 2
1273 internal.tasks_subdivision_dim = 2;
1274 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2] = 1;
1275 decomposition_done = 3;
1276 }
1277 else
1278 {
1279 internal.tasks_subdivision_dim = 3;
1280 decomposition_done = 3;
1281
1282 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2] = NTasks / (internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0] * internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1]);
1283
1284 if (internal.constrain_task_decomposition[2] > 0 &&
1285 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2] != internal.constrain_task_decomposition[2])
1286 {
1287 dprintf(VXERR, 0, "you specified a wrong fft decomposition: dim2 should be %d instead of %d\n", internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2], internal.constrain_task_decomposition[2]);
1288 return 1;
1289 }
1290 }
1291 }
1292 else // constrain_task_decomposition[1] > 0
1293 decomposition_done = 1;
1294
1295 // close if constrain_task_decomposition[1] > 0
1296
1297 } // close constrain_task_decomposition initial if
1298
1299 if (decomposition_done < 3)
1300 {
1301 // decomposition is still to be made or completed
1302 // we try to use as less dimensions as possible, maximizing contiguity
1303
1304 if (decomposition_done == 1)
1305 // only the first dimension has been specified in the param file,
1306 // but with a number of tasks smaller than NTasks
1307 {
1308 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1] = NTasks / internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0];
1309 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2] = 1;
1310 internal.tasks_subdivision_dim = 2;
1311
1312 if (NTasks % internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0])
1313 {
1314 dprintf(VXERR, 0, "you specified a wrong fft decomposition\n");
1315 return 1;
1316 }
1317 }
1318 else
1319 // no dimension has been constrained in the param file
1320 {
1321 // NOTE : no non-cubic grids, no multi-grids
1322
1323 int Ngrid = params.GridSize[0];
1324
1325 if (NTasks <= Ngrid)
1326 // prefer 1D decomposition for the most obvious case
1327 // that minimize communications in FFTs
1328 {
1329 internal.tasks_subdivision_dim = 1;
1330 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0] = NTasks;
1331 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2] = internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1] = 1;
1332 return 0;
1333 }
1334
1335 int Ngrid2 = Ngrid * Ngrid / (DECOMPOSITION_LIMIT_FACTOR_2D * DECOMPOSITION_LIMIT_FACTOR_2D);
1336
1337 if (NTasks <= Ngrid2)
1338 // check whether exact 2D pencil decomposition
1339 {
1340 unsigned GCD = gcd(Ngrid, NTasks);
1341 unsigned GCD_2 = gcd(Ngrid, (NTasks / GCD));
1342
1343 if (GCD * GCD_2 != NTasks)
1344 // no exact decomposition is possible,
1345 // revert to 1D decomposition
1346 {
1347 internal.tasks_subdivision_dim = 1;
1348 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0] = NTasks;
1349 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2] = internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1] = 1;
1350 return 0;
1351 }
1352 else
1353 {
1354 internal.tasks_subdivision_dim = 2;
1355 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0] = GCD;
1356 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1] = GCD_2;
1357 return 0;
1358 }
1359
1360 } // close if( NTasks < Ngrid2)
1361
1362 else
1363 // try 3d decomposition
1364 {
1365 int Ngrid_limit = Ngrid / DECOMPOSITION_LIMIT_FACTOR_2D;
1366
1367 unsigned GCD = gcd(Ngrid_limit, NTasks);
1368 unsigned GCD_2 = gcd(Ngrid_limit, (NTasks / GCD));
1369
1370 if (NTasks % (GCD * GCD_2))
1371 {
1372 dprintf(VXERR, 0, "3D decomposition is not possible\n");
1373 return 1;
1374 }
1375
1376 internal.tasks_subdivision_dim = 3;
1377 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[0] = GCD;
1378 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[1] = GCD_2;
1379
1380 internal.tasks_subdivision_3D[2] = NTasks / (GCD * GCD_2);
1381 return 0;
1382 }
1383 }
1384 }
1385
1386 return 0;
1387}
1388
1389int check_parameters_and_directives(void)
1390{
1391
1392#ifndef SNAPSHOT
1393 if (params.WriteTimelessSnapshot)
1394 {
1395 if (!ThisTask)
1396 printf("ERROR: to produce a snapshot you have to compile with SNAPSHOT directive\n");
1397 return 1;
1398 }
1399#endif
1400
1401#ifdef SNAPSHOT
1402
1403 static unsigned long long largest32 = (unsigned)1 << 31;
1404
1405#ifndef LONGIDS
1406 if ((params.WriteTimelessSnapshot) && MyGrids[0].Ntotal > largest32)
1407 {
1408 if (!ThisTask)
1409 printf("ERROR: with these many particles you need to compile with LONGIDS directive\n otherwise the snapshot IDs will be unreadable\n");
1410 return 1;
1411 }
1412#endif
1413
1414 /*
1415 if (params.WriteTimelessSnapshot)
1416 {
1417 unsigned long long BlockLength = MyGrids[0].Ntotal * 12 / (unsigned long long)params.NumFiles;
1418 if (BlockLength > largest32)
1419 {
1420 unsigned int NumFiles = (int)(MyGrids[0].Ntotal * 12 / largest32);
1421 if ((unsigned long long)(NumFiles * 12) * largest32 < MyGrids[0].Ntotal)
1422 ++NumFiles;
1423
1424 if (!ThisTask)
1425 {
1426 printf("ERROR: you need to write such a large snapshot with at least NumFiles=%d\n",NumFiles);
1427 }
1428 return 1;
1429 }
1430 }
1431 */
1432#endif
1433
1434 return 0;
1435}
1436
1437#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
1438#include "def_splines.h"
1439#define SMALLDIFF ((double)1.e-5)
1440#define TOLERANCE ((double)1.e-4)
1441#define MAXITER 20
1442// #define DEBUG
1443
1444double ThisRadius;
1445double Time;
1446
1447double IntegrandForSDDensVariance(double logk, void *radius)
1448{
1449 double k, w, D;
1450
1451 k = pow(10., logk);
1452 w = WindowFunction(k * ThisRadius);
1453 D = GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., k);
1454 return PowerSpectrum(k) * D * D * w * w * k * k * k / (2. * PI * PI);
1455}
1456
1457double IntegrandForSDDisplVariance(double logk, void *radius)
1458{
1459 double k, w, D;
1460
1461 k = pow(10., logk);
1462 w = WindowFunction(k * ThisRadius);
1463 D = GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., k);
1464 return PowerSpectrum(k) * D * D * w * w * k / (2. * PI * PI);
1465}
1466
1467double IntegrandForSDDispl2Variance(double logk, void *radius)
1468{
1469 double k, w, D;
1470
1471 k = pow(10., logk);
1472 w = WindowFunction(k * ThisRadius);
1473 D = GrowingMode_2LPT(1. / Time - 1., k);
1474 return PowerSpectrum(k) * D * D * w * w * k / (2. * PI * PI);
1475}
1476
1477double IntegrandForSDDispl31Variance(double logk, void *radius)
1478{
1479 double k, w, D;
1480
1481 k = pow(10., logk);
1482 w = WindowFunction(k * ThisRadius);
1483 D = GrowingMode_3LPT_1(1. / Time - 1., k);
1484 return PowerSpectrum(k) * D * D * w * w * k / (2. * PI * PI);
1485}
1486
1487double IntegrandForSDDispl32Variance(double logk, void *radius)
1488{
1489 double k, w, D;
1490
1491 k = pow(10., logk);
1492 w = WindowFunction(k * ThisRadius);
1493 D = GrowingMode_3LPT_2(1. / Time - 1., k);
1494 return PowerSpectrum(k) * D * D * w * w * k / (2. * PI * PI);
1495}
1496
1497double IntegrandForSDVelVariance(double logk, void *radius)
1498{
1499 double k, w, D, fo;
1500
1501 k = pow(10., logk);
1502 w = WindowFunction(k * ThisRadius);
1503 D = GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., k);
1504 fo = fomega(1. / Time - 1., k);
1505 return PowerSpectrum(k) * D * D * fo * fo * w * w * k / (2. * PI * PI);
1506}
1507
1508double IntegrandForSDVel2Variance(double logk, void *radius)
1509{
1510 double k, w, D, fo;
1511
1512 k = pow(10., logk);
1513 w = WindowFunction(k * ThisRadius);
1514 D = GrowingMode_2LPT(1. / Time - 1., k);
1515 fo = fomega_2LPT(1. / Time - 1., k);
1516 return PowerSpectrum(k) * D * D * fo * fo * w * w * k / (2. * PI * PI);
1517}
1518
1519double IntegrandForSDVel31Variance(double logk, void *radius)
1520{
1521 double k, w, D, fo;
1522
1523 k = pow(10., logk);
1524 w = WindowFunction(k * ThisRadius);
1525 D = GrowingMode_3LPT_1(1. / Time - 1., k);
1526 fo = fomega_3LPT_1(1. / Time - 1., k);
1527 return PowerSpectrum(k) * D * D * fo * fo * w * w * k / (2. * PI * PI);
1528}
1529
1530double IntegrandForSDVel32Variance(double logk, void *radius)
1531{
1532 double k, w, D, fo;
1533
1534 k = pow(10., logk);
1535 w = WindowFunction(k * ThisRadius);
1536 D = GrowingMode_3LPT_2(1. / Time - 1., k);
1537 fo = fomega_3LPT_2(1. / Time - 1., k);
1538 return PowerSpectrum(k) * D * D * fo * fo * w * w * k / (2. * PI * PI);
1539}
1540
1541int set_scaledep_GM()
1542{
1543 /* computes for each smoothing radius the k wavenumber at which the Fourier growing mode
1544 has the same time evolution of the rms of the density and displacement */
1545
1546 double *vector;
1547 gsl_function Function;
1548 int ismooth, i, Today, Z20, iter;
1549 double nyquist, normv, normGM1, normGM2, normGMm, logk1, logk2, logkmid, k1, k2, kmid,
1550 diff1, diff2, diffm, mindiff, result, error;
1551
1552#ifdef DEBUG
1553 gsl_function Function2, Function31, Function32;
1554 FILE *fd;
1555#endif
1556
1557#ifdef ELL_CLASSIC
1558 /* splines for inverse growing mode */
1559 SPLINE_INVGROW = (gsl_spline **)calloc(Smoothing.Nsmooth, sizeof(gsl_spline *));
1560 for (i = 0; i < Smoothing.Nsmooth; i++)
1561 SPLINE_INVGROW[i] = gsl_spline_alloc(gsl_interp_cspline, NBINS);
1562 ACCEL_INVGROW = (gsl_interp_accel **)calloc(Smoothing.Nsmooth, sizeof(gsl_interp_accel *));
1563 for (i = 0; i < Smoothing.Nsmooth; i++)
1564 ACCEL_INVGROW[i] = gsl_interp_accel_alloc();
1565#endif
1566
1567 Smoothing.Rad_GM = (double *)malloc(Smoothing.Nsmooth * sizeof(double));
1568 Smoothing.k_GM_dens = (double *)malloc(Smoothing.Nsmooth * sizeof(double));
1569 Smoothing.k_GM_displ = (double *)malloc(Smoothing.Nsmooth * sizeof(double));
1570 Smoothing.k_GM_vel = (double *)malloc(Smoothing.Nsmooth * sizeof(double));
1571
1572 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1573 if (pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]) > 1.0)
1574 break;
1575 Today = i - 1;
1576
1577 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1578 if (pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]) > 1. / 21.)
1579 break;
1580 Z20 = i - 1;
1581
1582 /***********/
1583 /* density */
1584 /***********/
1585 Function.function = &IntegrandForSDDensVariance;
1586 vector = (double *)malloc(NBINS * sizeof(double));
1587
1588 /* density (mass) variance is computed using Gaussian smoothing,
1589 the integral will be truncated at the Nyquist frequency */
1590 WindowFunctionType = 0;
1591 nyquist = NYQUIST * PI / params.InterPartDist;
1592
1593#ifdef DEBUG
1594 if (!ThisTask)
1595 fd = fopen("accuracy_scaledependent_dens.txt", "w");
1596#endif
1597
1598 /* cycle on smoothing radii */
1599 for (ismooth = 0; ismooth < Smoothing.Nsmooth; ismooth++)
1600 {
1601 /* here we use the (Gaussian) smoothing radii used in the rest of the code */
1602 ThisRadius = Smoothing.Radius[ismooth];
1603
1604 /* integrates the growing mode and stores in vector */
1605 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1606 {
1607 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1608 gsl_integration_qags(&Function, -4., nyquist, 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
1609 vector[i] = sqrt(result);
1610 }
1611 normv = vector[Today];
1612 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1613 vector[i] /= normv;
1614
1615 /* bisector search of the k that gives the best approximation to the growth rate */
1616 iter = 0;
1617
1618 /* first guess at the extremes in k */
1619 logk1 = LOGKMIN;
1620 logk2 = LOGKMIN + (NkBINS - 1) * DELTALOGK;
1621 k1 = pow(10., logk1);
1622 k2 = pow(10., logk2);
1623
1624 /* differences at the extremes in k */
1625 normGM1 = GrowingMode(0.0, k1);
1626 normGM2 = GrowingMode(0.0, k2);
1627 for (diff1 = diff2 = 0.0, i = Z20; i <= Today; i++)
1628 {
1629 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1630 diff1 += vector[i] - GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., k1) / normGM1;
1631 diff2 += vector[i] - GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., k2) / normGM2;
1632 }
1633 diff1 /= (double)NBINS;
1634 diff2 /= (double)NBINS;
1635
1636 /* k1 might be accurate enough */
1637 if (fabs(diff1) < SMALLDIFF)
1638 Smoothing.k_GM_dens[ismooth] = k1;
1639
1640 /* k2 might be accurate enough */
1641 else if (fabs(diff2) < SMALLDIFF)
1642 Smoothing.k_GM_dens[ismooth] = k2;
1643
1644 /* this is an unfortunate case: the two differences have the same sign
1645 and none is accurate enough. In this case the scale is set to the k that gives
1646 the smallest difference, and a warning is printed out */
1647 else if (diff1 * diff2 > 0)
1648 {
1649 if (!ThisTask)
1650 printf("WARNING in scale-dependent density growth rate for smoothing radius %d (%f): accuracy not guaranteed [diff1=%g - diff2=%g]\n",
1651 ismooth, Smoothing.Radius[ismooth], diff1, diff2);
1652 if (fabs(diff1) < fabs(diff2))
1653 Smoothing.k_GM_dens[ismooth] = logk1;
1654 else
1655 Smoothing.k_GM_dens[ismooth] = k2;
1656 }
1657 else
1658 {
1659 /* smallest difference */
1660 mindiff = fabs(diff1);
1661 mindiff = (fabs(diff2) < mindiff ? fabs(diff2) : mindiff);
1662
1663 /* iteration for bisector search */
1664 do
1665 {
1666
1667 /* midpoint in log space */
1668 logkmid = 0.5 * (logk1 + logk2);
1669 kmid = pow(10., logkmid);
1670
1671 /* difference at midpoint */
1672 normGMm = GrowingMode(0.0, kmid);
1673 for (diffm = 0.0, i = Z20; i <= Today; i++)
1674 {
1675 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1676 diffm += vector[i] - GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., kmid) / normGMm;
1677 }
1678 diffm /= (double)NBINS;
1679
1680 /* update of mindiff if necessary */
1681 mindiff = (fabs(diffm) < mindiff ? fabs(diffm) : mindiff);
1682
1683 /* new extreme points */
1684 if (diff1 * diffm > 0)
1685 {
1686 logk1 = logkmid;
1687 diff1 = diffm;
1688 }
1689 else
1690 {
1691 logk2 = logkmid;
1692 diff2 = diffm;
1693 }
1694 ++iter;
1695 } while (fabs(mindiff) > SMALLDIFF && iter <= MAXITER);
1696
1697 Smoothing.k_GM_dens[ismooth] = kmid;
1698 }
1699
1700#ifdef DEBUG
1701 printf("density, smoothing %d, iter=%d\n", ismooth, iter);
1702 if (!ThisTask)
1703 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1704 {
1705 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1706 fprintf(fd, " %d %g %g %g %g %g\n",
1707 ismooth, Smoothing.Radius[ismooth], Smoothing.k_GM_dens[ismooth],
1708 Time, vector[i], GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_dens[ismooth]) / GrowingMode(0.0, Smoothing.k_GM_dens[ismooth]));
1709 }
1710#endif
1711
1712#ifdef ELL_CLASSIC
1713 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1714 vector[i] = log10(vector[i]);
1715 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE_INVGROW[ismooth], vector, &(SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[0]), NBINS);
1716#endif
1717 }
1718#ifdef DEBUG
1719 if (!ThisTask)
1720 fclose(fd);
1721#endif
1722
1723 /*****************/
1724 /* displacements */
1725 /*****************/
1726 Function.function = &IntegrandForSDDisplVariance;
1727#ifdef DEBUG
1728 Function2.function = &IntegrandForSDDispl2Variance;
1729 Function31.function = &IntegrandForSDDispl31Variance;
1730 Function32.function = &IntegrandForSDDispl32Variance;
1731#endif
1732
1733 /* displacement variance is computed using Top-Hat smoothing,
1734 the integral will be again truncated at the Nyquist frequency */
1735 WindowFunctionType = 2;
1736
1737#ifdef DEBUG
1738 if (!ThisTask)
1739 fd = fopen("accuracy_scaledependent_disp.txt", "w");
1740#endif
1741
1742 double Largest = SizeForMass(pow(10., mf.mmax));
1743 /* cycle on smoothing radii */
1744 for (ismooth = 0; ismooth < Smoothing.Nsmooth; ismooth++)
1745 {
1746 ThisRadius = Smoothing.Rad_GM[ismooth] = Largest * (Smoothing.Nsmooth - 1 - ismooth) / (double)(Smoothing.Nsmooth - 1);
1747
1748 /* integrates the growing mode and stores in vector */
1749 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1750 {
1751 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1752 gsl_integration_qags(&Function, -4., nyquist, 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
1753 vector[i] = sqrt(result);
1754 }
1755 normv = vector[Today];
1756 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1757 vector[i] /= normv;
1758
1759 /* bisector search of the k that gives the best approximation to the growth rate */
1760 iter = 0;
1761
1762 /* first guess at the extremes in k */
1763 logk1 = LOGKMIN;
1764 logk2 = LOGKMIN + (NkBINS - 1) * DELTALOGK;
1765 k1 = pow(10., logk1);
1766 k2 = pow(10., logk2);
1767
1768 /* differences at the extremes in k */
1769 normGM1 = GrowingMode(0.0, k1);
1770 normGM2 = GrowingMode(0.0, k2);
1771 for (diff1 = diff2 = 0.0, i = Z20; i <= Today; i++)
1772 {
1773 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1774 diff1 += vector[i] - GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., k1) / normGM1;
1775 diff2 += vector[i] - GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., k2) / normGM2;
1776 }
1777 diff1 /= (double)NBINS;
1778 diff2 /= (double)NBINS;
1779
1780 /* k1 might be accurate enough */
1781 if (fabs(diff1) < SMALLDIFF)
1782 Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth] = k1;
1783
1784 /* k2 might be accurate enough */
1785 else if (fabs(diff2) < SMALLDIFF)
1786 Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth] = k2;
1787
1788 /* this is an unfortunate case: the two differences have the same sign
1789 and none is accurate enough. In this case the scale is set to the k that gives
1790 the smallest difference, and a warning is printed out */
1791 else if (diff1 * diff2 > 0)
1792 {
1793 if (!ThisTask)
1794 printf("WARNING in scale-dependent density growth rate for smoothing radius %d (%f): accuracy not guaranteed [diff1=%g - diff2=%g]\n",
1795 ismooth, Smoothing.Rad_GM[ismooth], diff1, diff2);
1796 if (fabs(diff1) < fabs(diff2))
1797 Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth] = k1;
1798
1799 else
1800 Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth] = k2;
1801 }
1802 else
1803 {
1804 /* smallest difference */
1805 mindiff = fabs(diff1);
1806 mindiff = (fabs(diff2) < mindiff ? fabs(diff2) : mindiff);
1807
1808 /* iteration for bisector search */
1809 do
1810 {
1811
1812 /* midpoint in log space */
1813 logkmid = 0.5 * (logk1 + logk2);
1814 kmid = pow(10., logkmid);
1815
1816 /* difference at midpoint */
1817 normGMm = GrowingMode(0.0, kmid);
1818 for (diffm = 0.0, i = Z20; i <= Today; i++)
1819 {
1820 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1821 diffm += vector[i] - GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., kmid) / normGMm;
1822 }
1823 diffm /= (double)NBINS;
1824
1825 /* update of mindiff if necessary */
1826 mindiff = (fabs(diffm) < mindiff ? fabs(diffm) : mindiff);
1827
1828 /* new extreme points */
1829 if (diff1 * diffm > 0)
1830 {
1831 logk1 = logkmid;
1832 diff1 = diffm;
1833 }
1834 else
1835 {
1836 logk2 = logkmid;
1837 diff2 = diffm;
1838 }
1839 ++iter;
1840 } while (fabs(mindiff) > SMALLDIFF && iter <= MAXITER);
1841
1842 Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth] = kmid;
1843 }
1844
1845#ifdef DEBUG
1846 printf("displacements, smoothing %d, iter=%d\n", ismooth, iter);
1847 Time = 1.0;
1848 gsl_integration_qags(&Function2, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
1849 normGM1 = sqrt(result);
1850 gsl_integration_qags(&Function31, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
1851 normGM2 = sqrt(result);
1852 gsl_integration_qags(&Function32, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
1853 normGMm = sqrt(result);
1854 if (!ThisTask)
1855 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1856 {
1857 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1858 fprintf(fd, " %d %g %g %g %g %g",
1859 ismooth, Smoothing.Rad_GM[ismooth], Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth],
1860 Time, vector[i], GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth]) / GrowingMode(0.0, Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth]));
1861 gsl_integration_qags(&Function2, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
1862 fprintf(fd, " %g %g", sqrt(result) / normGM1, GrowingMode_2LPT(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth]) / GrowingMode_2LPT(0.0, Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth]));
1863 gsl_integration_qags(&Function31, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
1864 fprintf(fd, " %g %g", sqrt(result) / normGM2, GrowingMode_3LPT_1(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth]) / GrowingMode_3LPT_1(0.0, Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth]));
1865 gsl_integration_qags(&Function32, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
1866 fprintf(fd, " %g %g\n", sqrt(result) / normGMm, GrowingMode_3LPT_2(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth]) / GrowingMode_3LPT_2(0.0, Smoothing.k_GM_displ[ismooth]));
1867 }
1868#endif
1869 }
1870
1871#ifdef DEBUG
1872 if (!ThisTask)
1873 fclose(fd);
1874#endif
1875
1876 /**************/
1877 /* velocities */
1878 /**************/
1879 Function.function = &IntegrandForSDVelVariance;
1880#ifdef DEBUG
1881 Function2.function = &IntegrandForSDVel2Variance;
1882 Function31.function = &IntegrandForSDVel31Variance;
1883 Function32.function = &IntegrandForSDVel32Variance;
1884#endif
1885
1886 /* displacement variance is computed using Top-Hat smoothing,
1887 the integral will be again truncated at the Nyquist frequency */
1888 WindowFunctionType = 2;
1889
1890#ifdef DEBUG
1891 if (!ThisTask)
1892 fd = fopen("accuracy_scaledependent_velo.txt", "w");
1893#endif
1894
1895 /* cycle on smoothing radii */
1896 for (ismooth = 0; ismooth < Smoothing.Nsmooth; ismooth++)
1897 {
1898 ThisRadius = Smoothing.Rad_GM[ismooth];
1899
1900 /* integrates the growing mode and stores in vector */
1901 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1902 {
1903 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1904 gsl_integration_qags(&Function, -4., nyquist, 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
1905 vector[i] = sqrt(result);
1906 }
1907 normv = vector[Today];
1908 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1909 vector[i] /= normv;
1910
1911 /* bisector search of the k that gives the best approximation to the growth rate */
1912 iter = 0;
1913
1914 /* first guess at the extremes in k */
1915 logk1 = LOGKMIN;
1916 logk2 = LOGKMIN + (NkBINS - 1) * DELTALOGK;
1917 k1 = pow(10., logk1);
1918 k2 = pow(10., logk2);
1919
1920 /* differences at the extremes in k */
1921 normGM1 = GrowingMode(0.0, k1) * fomega(0.0, k1);
1922 normGM2 = GrowingMode(0.0, k2) * fomega(0.0, k2);
1923 for (diff1 = diff2 = 0.0, i = Z20; i <= Today; i++)
1924 {
1925 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1926 diff1 += vector[i] - GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., k1) * fomega(1. / Time - 1, k1) / normGM1;
1927 diff2 += vector[i] - GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., k2) * fomega(1. / Time - 1, k1) / normGM2;
1928 }
1929 diff1 /= (double)NBINS;
1930 diff2 /= (double)NBINS;
1931
1932 /* k1 might be accurate enough */
1933 if (fabs(diff1) < SMALLDIFF)
1934 Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth] = k1;
1935
1936 /* k2 might be accurate enough */
1937 else if (fabs(diff2) < SMALLDIFF)
1938 Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth] = k2;
1939
1940 /* this is an unfortunate case: the two differences have the same sign
1941 and none is accurate enough. In this case the scale is set to the k that gives
1942 the smallest difference, and a warning is printed out */
1943 else if (diff1 * diff2 > 0)
1944 {
1945 if (!ThisTask)
1946 printf("WARNING in scale-dependent density growth rate for smoothing radius %d (%f): accuracy not guaranteed [diff1=%g - diff2=%g]\n",
1947 ismooth, Smoothing.Rad_GM[ismooth], diff1, diff2);
1948 if (fabs(diff1) < fabs(diff2))
1949 Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth] = k1;
1950 else
1951 Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth] = k2;
1952 }
1953 else
1954 {
1955 /* smallest difference */
1956 mindiff = fabs(diff1);
1957 mindiff = (fabs(diff2) < mindiff ? fabs(diff2) : mindiff);
1958
1959 /* iteration for bisector search */
1960 do
1961 {
1962
1963 /* midpoint in log space */
1964 logkmid = 0.5 * (logk1 + logk2);
1965 kmid = pow(10., logkmid);
1966
1967 /* difference at midpoint */
1968 normGMm = GrowingMode(0.0, kmid) * fomega(0.0, kmid);
1969 for (diffm = 0.0, i = Z20; i <= Today; i++)
1970 {
1971 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
1972 diffm += vector[i] - GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., kmid) * fomega(1. / Time - 1., kmid) / normGMm;
1973 }
1974 diffm /= (double)NBINS;
1975
1976 /* update of mindiff if necessary */
1977 mindiff = (fabs(diffm) < mindiff ? fabs(diffm) : mindiff);
1978
1979 /* new extreme points */
1980 if (diff1 * diffm > 0)
1981 {
1982 logk1 = logkmid;
1983 diff1 = diffm;
1984 }
1985 else
1986 {
1987 logk2 = logkmid;
1988 diff2 = diffm;
1989 }
1990 ++iter;
1991 } while (fabs(mindiff) > SMALLDIFF && iter <= MAXITER);
1992
1993 Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth] = kmid;
1994 }
1995
1996#ifdef DEBUG
1997 printf("velocities, smoothing %d, iter=%d\n", ismooth, iter);
1998 Time = 1.0;
1999 gsl_integration_qags(&Function2, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
2000 normGM1 = sqrt(result);
2001 gsl_integration_qags(&Function31, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
2002 normGM2 = sqrt(result);
2003 gsl_integration_qags(&Function32, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
2004 normGMm = sqrt(result);
2005 if (!ThisTask)
2006 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
2007 {
2008 Time = pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]);
2009 fprintf(fd, " %d %g %g %g %g %g",
2010 ismooth, Smoothing.Rad_GM[ismooth], Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth],
2011 Time, vector[i], GrowingMode(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) * fomega(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) / GrowingMode(0., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) / fomega(0., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]));
2012
2013 gsl_integration_qags(&Function2, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
2014 fprintf(fd, " %g %g", sqrt(result) / normGM1, GrowingMode_2LPT(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) * fomega_2LPT(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) / GrowingMode_2LPT(0., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) / fomega_2LPT(0.0, Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]));
2015
2016 gsl_integration_qags(&Function31, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
2017 fprintf(fd, " %g %g", sqrt(result) / normGM2, GrowingMode_3LPT_1(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) * fomega_3LPT_1(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) / GrowingMode_3LPT_1(0., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) / fomega_3LPT_1(0., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]));
2018
2019 gsl_integration_qags(&Function32, -4., 2., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
2020 fprintf(fd, " %g %g\n", sqrt(result) / normGMm, GrowingMode_3LPT_2(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) * fomega_3LPT_2(1. / Time - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) / GrowingMode_3LPT_2(0., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]) / fomega_3LPT_2(0., Smoothing.k_GM_vel[ismooth]));
2021 }
2022#endif
2023 }
2024
2025#ifdef DEBUG
2026 if (!ThisTask)
2027 fclose(fd);
2028#endif
2029
2030 free(vector);
2031
2032 WindowFunctionType = 2;
2033 return 0;
2034}
2035
2036#endif
2037
2038void greetings(void)
2039{
2040 /* This is a list of messages to declare the most relevant precompiler directives in the stdout */
2041
2042 if (!ThisTask)
2043 {
2044 printf("[%s] This is pinocchio V5.1, running on %d MPI tasks\n\n", fdate(), NTasks);
2045#ifdef _OPENMP
2046 printf("Using %d OpenMP threads\n", internal.nthreads_omp);
2047#endif
2048
2049#ifdef USE_FFT_THREADS
2050 printf("Using threaded-FFTs\n");
2051#endif
2052#ifdef TWO_LPT
2053#ifndef THREE_LPT
2054 printf("Using 2LPT displacements\n");
2055#else
2056 printf("Using 3LPT displacements\n");
2057#endif
2058#else
2059 printf("Using Zeldovich displacements\n");
2060#endif
2061#ifdef PLC
2062 printf("Performing PLC construction\n");
2063#else
2064 printf("PLC reconstruction is switched off\n");
2065#endif
2066#ifdef NORADIATION
2067 printf("Radiation is not included in the Friedmann equations\n");
2068#else
2069 printf("Radiation is included in the Friedmann equations\n");
2070#endif
2071#ifdef ELL_CLASSIC
2072 printf("Ellipsoidal collapse will be computed as in Monaco (1995)\n");
2073#endif
2074#ifdef ELL_SNG
2075 printf("Ellipsoidal collapse will be computed as in Nadkarni-Ghosh (2015)\n");
2076#endif
2077#ifdef TABULATED_CT
2078 printf("Numerical integration of ellipsoidal collapse will be tabulated\n");
2079#endif
2080#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
2081 printf("Using classic fragmentation\n");
2082#else
2083 printf("Using v5 fragmentation\n");
2084#endif
2085#ifdef WHITENOISE
2086#error WHITENOISE is not implemented yet
2087 printf("Initial conditions will be read from a white noise file\n");
2088#endif
2089#ifdef SNAPSHOT
2090 printf("Writing of snapshots enabled\n");
2091#ifdef LONGIDS
2092 printf("IDs in the snapshot will be long long\n");
2093#endif
2094#endif
2095#ifdef LIGHT_OUTPUT
2096 printf("Catalogs will be written in the light version\n");
2097#endif
2098#ifdef DOUBLE_PRECISION_PRODUCTS
2099 printf("Products in double precision\n");
2100#endif
2101#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
2102 printf("This version of the code works with scale-dependent growing modes\n");
2103#ifdef MOD_GRAV_FR
2104 printf("Scales will range from %10g to %10g 1/Mpc, in %d steps\n",
2105 0.0, pow(10., LOGKMIN + (NkBINS - 1) * DELTALOGK), NkBINS);
2106 printf("Gravity will be given by Hu-Sawicki f(R) with f_R0=%7g\n", FR0);
2107#endif
2108#ifdef READ_PK_TABLE
2109 printf("Scales will range from %10g to %10g 1/Mpc, in %d steps\n",
2110 pow(10., LOGKMIN), pow(10., LOGKMIN + (NkBINS - 1) * DELTALOGK), NkBINS);
2111 printf("Scale-dependent growth rates will be worked out from CAMB P(k) files\n");
2112#ifdef READ_PK_TABLE
2113 printf("The power spectrum will include only dark matter + baryon fluctuations, excluding neutrinos (if present)\n");
2114#else
2115 printf("The power spectrum will include TOTAL matter fluctuations, including neutrinos (if present)\n");
2116#endif
2117#endif
2118#endif
2119
2120 printf("\n");
2121 }
2122}
